Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; regime labels shifted one day
Notes: Benchmark regime map. QQQ buy-and-hold had strong overall return, but regime summaries showed major trend sensitivity: uptrend returns were strong while downtrend returns were nearly flat with high volatility.

Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; regime labels shifted one day
Signal Rule: Hold QQQ when trailing 60-day momentum is positive; otherwise hold cash
Notes: Momentum reduced drawdown and improved Sharpe versus buy-and-hold, but gave up raw return and net profit. Regime summary suggests momentum improved behavior in downtrends and low-volatility regimes, while giving up significant upside in high-volatility and uptrend periods.

Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; regime labels shifted one day
Signal Rule: Hold QQQ when trailing 60-day momentum is positive and volatility regime is low; otherwise hold cash
Notes: Low-volatility filter reduced exposure and drawdown but weakened return and risk-adjusted performance materially versus EXP-002. The filter removed all high-volatility exposure, but this also removed meaningful return opportunities.

Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; regime labels shifted one day
Signal Rule: Hold QQQ when trailing 60-day momentum is positive and trend regime is uptrend; otherwise hold cash
Notes: Uptrend filter reduced drawdown versus plain momentum and performed better than the strict low-volatility filter, but still gave up too much return and Sharpe versus EXP-002. Trend filter was less damaging than volatility filter but did not improve overall strategy quality.

Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; regime labels shifted one day
Signal Rule: Hold QQQ when trailing 60-day momentum is positive, volatility regime is low, and trend regime is uptrend; otherwise hold cash
Notes: Combined low-volatility uptrend filter was very restrictive and produced weak return and risk-adjusted performance. It preserved drawdown control but underperformed plain momentum, uptrend-filtered momentum, and buy-and-hold by a wide margin.

Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; regime labels shifted one day
Signal Rule: Hold QQQ when trailing 120-day momentum is positive; otherwise hold cash
Notes: 120-day momentum remained positive and had lower turnover than 60-day momentum, supporting partial robustness of the plain momentum idea. However, it had a larger drawdown and lower QuantConnect Sharpe than EXP-002, so the 60-day version remains stronger so far.

Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; regime labels shifted one day
Signal Rule: Hold QQQ when trailing 20-day momentum is positive; otherwise hold cash
Notes: The faster 20-day signal produced the strongest CAGR and QuantConnect Sharpe among the tested momentum lookbacks, but also the most orders, highest fees, and a larger drawdown than the 60-day baseline. Daily-bar orders were converted to MarketOnOpen orders, so the synthetic regime return series and actual QuantConnect portfolio have a timing mismatch that must be corrected before the direct comparison experiment.

Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; prior-day regime labels; actual returns from daily portfolio value
Signal Rule: Calculate 60-day momentum after the close and schedule the next rebalance after the following market open; QuantConnect converted daily-resolution orders to MarketOnClose
Notes: Actual custom cumulative return of 155.01% reconciled with QuantConnect net profit of 155.007%, validating portfolio-value regime attribution. However, daily-resolution orders scheduled at 09:31 were automatically converted to MarketOnClose, so the observed execution was same-day close rather than the stated next-open rule.

Regime Rule: 20-day realized volatility vs rolling 252-day median; 200-day moving average trend; prior-day regime labels; actual returns from daily portfolio value
Signal Rule: Calculate 60-day momentum after the daily close and submit an explicit MarketOnOpen order for the next session
Notes: Explicit MarketOnOpen execution produced no order warnings and the custom actual cumulative return of 125.38% reconciled with QuantConnect net profit of 125.381%. The synthetic close-to-close series overstated return by 13.71 percentage points, confirming that next-open execution materially changes the strategy path.
